mappings.rationaleAI: Covers the nervous system's structure and function including reflex arcs, synaptic transmission, sensory and motor pathways, and the autonomic nervous system -- directly relevant to the nervous system's role in receiving information, processing it, and initiating responses.

Hormones covers the endocrine system's chemical messengers including adrenaline, oestrogen, testosterone, and feedback loops involving the hypothalamus and pituitary -- directly relevant to hormonal influences on behaviour and interaction of endocrine and nervous systems.

Echolocation in bats and dolphins illustrates stereotyped behavioral responses to environmental stimuli, neurological processing of sensory information, and the independent evolution of echolocation as evidence of genetic determination of behaviour.

Circadian rhythms govern sleep-wake patterns, hormone release, and metabolism through molecular feedback loops and the suprachiasmatic nucleus -- directly relevant to photoperiodism, endocrine-nervous system interaction, and hormonal influences on behaviour.

Genetic determinism addresses the debate over genetic versus environmental determination of behaviour, twin studies, and behavioral genetics -- directly relevant to evidence of genetic determination of behaviour and the influence of experience.

Human nature covers the nature-nurture debate, behaviorism versus nativism, twin studies, and gene-environment interaction in epigenetics -- directly relevant to both genetic determination of behaviour and the influence of experience.

The Eye covers the anatomy and mechanism of visual perception and visual processing in the brain, directly relevant to the nervous system's role in receiving sensory information and initiating appropriate responses.

Perception and the senses covers how the brain processes sensory information from multiple senses, the neuroscience of visual and auditory processing, and multisensory integration -- directly relevant to the nervous system receiving and processing information.

Behavioural ecology covers optimal foraging theory, kin selection, and game theory applied to animal strategies -- relevant to evolutionary shaping of behaviour and evidence of genetic determination of behavioural patterns.
